persona.the_one_thing: Dongwon Sung is a technically-trained VC (Data Scientist at SK hynix → AI Research Fellow at SK innovation → Principal at KIP) who built his reputation on a contrarian deep-tech bet — so walk in leading with the actual AI architecture and a differentiated thesis, not a market-narrative deck; he will evaluate the technical 'why.' (High confidence; basis: sourced career history s2 + first-person posts s27/s32.)
persona.snapshot: Dongwon Sung (LinkedIn shows the name 'Dongwon Ellie Sung') is a Principal at Korea Investment Partners — KIP, the VC/PE arm of Korea Investment Holdings, managing 58 funds and ~US$4B AUM (s4). The path here is unusually technical for a VC: Statistical Consultant at UIUC, Data Scientist at SK hynix (2018–2020), then AI Research Fellow at SK innovation (2020–2022) before joining KIP in April 2022 (s2). Public activity centers on AI and deep tech — quantum, on-device/edge AI, inference hardware, and manufacturing AI.
persona.whats_new: In June 2026 Sung posted that Quantinuum's IPO was a personal first exit, framing it as a contrarian quantum bet 'when no one in Korea was willing to look at quantum' that got vindicated (s27). In March 2026 Sung was billed to speak at a KIP × Microsoft 'Synergy Club' session on global AI trends and how startups scale globally (s32).

recent_activity.what: Posted that Quantinuum's public listing was a first exit and a vindicated contrarian quantum call
Amplified ENERZAi's 'AI on Edge' on-device AI seminar
Amplified NeuReality's NR-NEXUS 'token factory OS' / inference-hardware narrative
Congratulated QEDMA's Asif Sinay on the Q-Net Award (quantum)
Amplified InterX passing KOSDAQ technology evaluation for a 2026 manufacturing-AI IPO
Billed to speak at KIP × Microsoft 'Synergy Club' on global AI trends / scaling startups globally

top_moves.fact: Career runs Data Scientist (SK hynix) → AI Research Fellow (SK innovation) → Principal at KIP (s2).
top_moves.inference: He can read model/agent architecture, not just slides — and likely defaults to probing it.
top_moves.move: Open with how Orbt's agents are built and why the reasoning holds up; have an architecture answer ready before the market-size slide. Show the engineering, then the business model.

top_moves.fact: On his first exit (Quantinuum) he wrote: 'we invested when no one in Korea was willing to look at quantum. It was a contrarian call' (s27).
top_moves.inference: He is rewarded by — and proud of — non-consensus theses, and is wary of crowded me-too plays.
top_moves.move: Lead with what is genuinely non-obvious or contrarian about Orbt (AI agents replacing parts of cross-border consulting), not with how the AI-consulting category is heating up. Name the bet he'd be making.

top_moves.fact: Billed to speak on 'how startups can scale globally' at the KIP × Microsoft session (s32).
top_moves.inference: He frames Korean startups through a cross-border / global expansion thesis.
top_moves.move: Position Orbt's Korean F&B & cosmetics cross-border GTM not as a niche service but as a repeatable global-scaling engine — the exact thesis he's publicly speaking to.

non_obvious.insight: His public amplifications cluster on deep tech and infrastructure — quantum (s27, s30), on-device/edge AI (s28), inference hardware (s29), manufacturing AI (s31) — not application-layer SaaS. Orbt is an application/agent layer, so expect a defensibility probe: 'what's the moat once the model commoditizes?' Pre-empt it; don't be caught framing Orbt as a thin wrapper.

likely_objections.objection: Application-layer AI consulting — what's the moat once foundation models commoditize the agent layer?
likely_objections.counter: Show what's proprietary: accumulated cross-border playbooks/data, agent orchestration over Slack delivery, and execution outcomes for real brands — not the model itself.

likely_objections.objection: His public interest skews deep-tech/infra, not services — is this 'venture-scale' or a consulting shop?
likely_objections.counter: Frame Orbt as a repeatable, agent-driven product that compounds with each country/brand, with the unit economics and traction to back it.

likely_objections.objection: Why is Claude-native a durable edge vs. switching models?
likely_objections.counter: Tie the Claude-native choice to a concrete technical/quality reason for the agent reasoning — present it as an engineering decision, not a marketing line. (Do not assume he already uses Claude.)

chemistry.overall_read: A technical founder meeting an unusually technical VC — both anchor identity in demonstrated rigor over narrative, so the conversation should click on substance fast; the main tension is thesis-fit, since orbt's applied consumer-goods AI sits adjacent to (not inside) Sung's deep-tech/infrastructure comfort zone. Moderate confidence given thinner data on his side.

mbti_compat.read: Two strategy-first N-T types; INTJ brings the build-depth, ENTJ pushes scale and decisiveness — they tend to respect each other and move quickly, with the ENTJ driving tempo. illustrative · not clinical
mbti_compat.label: directional

complementary_traits.them_signal: Principal at a ~US$4B-AUM VC who frames perspectives on how startups scale globally
complementary_traits.why_it_helps: Sunwoo can answer 'how does it actually work' to a depth most founders can't, and Sung can map that into a global-scaling and funding thesis — each fills the other's blind spot.

complementary_traits.trait: Conviction style: rigor-first vs. contrarian bet
complementary_traits.me_signal: Trusts durable partnerships over flashy new ideas; wants reasoning to hold up
complementary_traits.them_signal: Backed quantum 'when no one in Korea was willing to look at it' — rewards non-consensus conviction
complementary_traits.why_it_helps: If Sunwoo can frame orbt's intelligence engine as a defensible non-consensus bet (not just a services play), it directly meets Sung's deal-making instinct.

potential_friction.point: Thesis-fit gap: applied consumer-goods AI vs. infrastructure-grade deep tech
potential_friction.me_signal: orbt runs strategy and ad creative for F&B and cosmetics brands, delivered via Slack — applied/services-flavored AI
potential_friction.them_signal: Amplifies quantum, inference hardware, on-device/edge and manufacturing AI — infrastructure deep tech
potential_friction.how_to_navigate: Lead with the 'intelligence-without-search / decision-testing' engine as the defensible tech core, not the consulting wrapper; show why the IP, not the service, is the moat.

potential_friction.point: Both default to driving / being right
potential_friction.me_signal: INTJ, frames self by rigor and being the technical authority
potential_friction.them_signal: ENTJ investor who frames a vindicated contrarian call as personal conviction
potential_friction.how_to_navigate: Treat probing questions as a quality test, not a challenge; concede unknowns explicitly — admitting one open risk will buy more credibility than defending everything.

interaction_simulation.opening_30s: Skip the pitch theater — open on the technical substance: 'orbt's engine does decision-testing for D2C without search.' His data-science/AI-research background means he'll engage on architecture in seconds.
interaction_simulation.likely_arc: Fast convergence on AI mechanics and rigor, then Sung steers toward scale, defensibility, and global expansion — where Sunwoo's cross-border GTM and Meet the Drapers / pre-seed proof points reinforce the case.
interaction_simulation.where_it_could_stall: If it reads as a consulting/services business rather than a deep-tech product, Sung's deep-tech filter may cool; also if both lock into authority mode and stop asking questions.
interaction_simulation.how_to_adapt: Frame orbt's IP as the non-consensus bet, name one hard open problem honestly, and tie the cross-border product explicitly to his 'how startups scale globally' frame.

honest_gaps: chemistry is a low-confidence inference from public text, not a relationship prediction
Sung's side is thinner (coverage 0.55) and his Openness/communication style is only weakly inferable
